title: bupleurum olympicum var. oeneum
intro: Bupleurum olympicum var. oeneum, also known as a variety of the Hare's Ear or Ox-Tongue, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family, known for its medicinal properties. This plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immune-boosting effects. The medicinal properties of Bupleurum olympicum var. oeneum have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, and have been found to have antioxidant, antipyretic, and expectorant properties, contributing to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and relieving respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in combination with other medicinal herbs, and can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions.
